summary: Magical Spanish Jam suite, strong guitar, vibrant second set
blurb: Features a high‑energy first set highlighted by Stagger Lee and Jack Straw, then erupts into a second‑set jam marathon—Spanish Jam flowing into Goin’ Down The Road Feeling Bad, Throwing Stones and Not Fade Away. Jerry’s guitar shines, Phil’s bass is turned up to 11, and Vince adds vivid color on keys.
review: On March 24, 1993 the Dead took the Dean Smith Center in Chapel Hill by storm. The first set rolled along with tight renditions of Jack Straw, Stagger Lee and a blues‑y Wang Dang Doodle that set the mood for an inspired second half. Once the band launched into Playing In The Band, they never looked back: a seamless segue into Crazy Fingers gave way to a soaring jam that birthed one of the night’s high points—Spanish Jam spilling into Goin’ Down The Road Feeling Bad, then exploding through Throwing Stones and a jubilant Not Fade Away. The encore closed with a sparkling Lucy In The Sky With Diamonds. Jerry Garcia was on fire, delivering some of his most lyrical leads of the ’90s, while Phil Lesh turned the bass up “to 11,” anchoring the improvisations. Vince Welnick’s keyboards added bright color throughout, and both Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann kept a propulsive rhythm that drove the band’s energy to the roof.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Six of eight recordings are marked positive and praise the same key moments—Spanish Jam suite, Throwing Stones, Not Fade Away, and Jerry’s guitar work. Two mixed reviews note minor digi‑noise but still highlight the strong playing. The consistent enthusiasm across multiple sources warrants a strong positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard capture with crystal‑clear mix and no drop‑outs; reviewers cite crisp audio and highlight all the show’s standout songs.

key_highlights: Spanish Jam suite that rolls into Goin’ Down The Road Feeling Bad, Throwing Stones and Not Fade Away
Jerry Garcia’s soaring guitar on Stagger Lee, Crazy Fingers and throughout the jam marathon
Phil Lesh’s bass turned up to 11, providing a powerful low‑end foundation
Lucy In The Sky With Diamonds encore shines as a perfect closing
song_highlights: Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad
Lucy In The Sky With Diamonds

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia delivers some of his best ’90s playing—powerful, lyrical leads on Stagger Lee, Crazy Fingers and the extended jam sequence, staying in top form throughout.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh’s bass is turned up to 11, anchoring the band with a deep, resonant tone; notable noodling on Not Fade Away and a standout performance on Box of Rain.
band_performance.Bob: Weir provides solid rhythm work, especially on Let It Grow and maintains steady support across the set.
band_performance.Vince: Welnick’s keyboards add bright color and texture to the jams, earning repeated praise for being “pretty damn good” and enhancing the improvisational flow.
band_performance.Mickey: Hart contributes tasteful percussive accents that complement the dual‑drum interplay without overpowering the music.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann lays down a solid, driving beat; his post‑drums section fuels the energy of the Spanish Jam suite.

must_listen_sequences: Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad
Lucy In The Sky With Diamonds
